title: elaeocarpus grumosus
intro: Elaeocarpus grumosus, also known as the Rough-leaved Blueberry Tree or Blueberry Ash, is a plant species native to Australia and New Caledonia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, leaves, and roots, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus grumosus include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic for wounds and infections. Elaeocarpus grumosus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to reap its health benefits.
